english: kyis (kyis) Dzl. 20, 14; kyod (khyod) daṅ (dang) γnyen-zlá (gnyen zla) min (min) I am not allied with you by marriage, with you I am not on terms of affinity. — γnyen-śál (gnyen shal) (?) reconciliation C. — γnyen-bśés (gnyen bshes) relatives and friends, also separately: kyód-la (khyod la) γnyen (gnyen) med (med) bśés (bshes) kyaṅ (kyang) med (med) Mil.

c. accus. to take pains with, to take care of, to provide for, to try to get; to procure, to acquire, γnyer (gnyer) byéd-pa (byed pa) id.; as a sbst. Tar. 165, 22: the procurer, provider Schf.; gen. in conjunction with don (don) in various ways, as: bdag (bdag) don (don) źig (zhig) γnyér-te (gnyer te) as I have to look after a business Dzl. 20, 7; don (don) γnyér-ba (gnyer ba) to earn money; dón-du (don du) γnyér-ba (gnyer ba) c. accus., rarely c. dat.: to provide for, to strive to procure, nor (nor) dón-du (don du) γnyér-ba (gnyer ba) to endeavour to make money, frq.; yo-byád (yo byad) dón-du (don du) γnyér-ba-rnams (gnyer ba rnams) people who desired to have goods Tar. 169, hence don-γnyér (don gnyer) exertion, effort, zeal, don-γnyér (don gnyer) čén-po (chen po) dgos (dgos) great exertions are necessary Mil.; in this sense prob. also Tar. 4, 8: earnest exertion (in investigating); don-γnyér (don gnyer) byéd-pa (byed pa) c. la to study, investigate (a thing) Glr.; don-γnyér-ċan (don gnyer can) 1. zealous, painstaking. 2. Sch. also: liked, welcome, mgron (mgron) a welcome guest. — dkon-γnyér (dkon gnyer) Tar. 183, 21, Schf.: administrator of valuable property; acc. to others: the first secular functionary of a gtsúg-lag-kaṅ (gtsug lag khang), about the same as bailiff (steward) of a convent, = lha-γnyér (lha gnyer) Georgi Alph. Tib. (in an edict); also the manager of the daily sacrifices (dgon-γnyér (dgon gnyer)?); slob-γnyér (slob gnyer) a student, čos-slob-γnyér (chos slob gnyer) a religious scholar (a student of theology) Mil., slob-γnyér (slob gnyer) gáṅ-du (gang du) bgyis (bgyis) where did you study? Mil. — gtad-γnyér (gtad gnyer) byéd-pa (byed pa) to trust (a person with), to intrust (a thing to) Glr.; čéd-du (ched du) γnyér-ba (gnyer ba), and rjés-su (rjes su) γnyér-ba (gnyer ba) v. čed (ched). — γnyér-ka (gnyer ka) attention, care, γnyér-ka (gnyer ka) byéd-pa (byed pa) ccg. to pay attention to, attend to, take care of Pth.; γnyér-ka (gnyer ka) gtád-pa (gtad pa) to commit (a thing) to a person's charge, to put a person in trust of Glr. — γnyér-pa (gnyer pa) farm-steward, in convents etc. — γnyér-byáṅ (gnyer byang) prob. = γnyér-ka (gnyer ka). — γnyér-tśaṅ (gnyer tshang) store-room, store-house, (if under the charge of a special γnyér-pa (gnyer pa)).

a fold of the skin, wrinkle Med.; γnyer-ma (gnyer ma) réṅs-pa (rengs pa) °gyur ('gyur) the wrinkles are made straight, are smoothed Stg.; γnyér-ma-ċan (gnyer ma can) wrinkled; kro-γnyér (khro gnyer) (भृकुटि) a frown, a severe or angry look v. kró-ba (khro ba); γnyér-ba (gnyer ba) to wrinkle, sna-góṅ (sna gong) γnyér-ba (gnyer ba) to knit the brows, to frown Pth.
to desire, to wish earnestly Cs. v. snyóg-pa (snyog pa).

strength, durability, stoutness of cloth etc., C. and W., γnyód-ċan (gnyod can) strong; γnyod-čúṅ (gnyod chung), γnyod-méd (gnyod med) weak; Lex. lus (lus) γnyod-čúṅ (gnyod chung) a weakly body or constitution.
to draw, stretch, strain C., W.
C. boat, skiff, wherry; mnyán-pa (mnyan pa) boat-man, ferry-man.
1. = nyán-pa (nyan pa) Dzl. etc. — 2. v. mnyan (mnyan).
a town in the northern part of Oudh.

col. "nyám-po" ("nyam po"), 1. like, alike, equal, same, mnyám-po (mnyam po) yód (yod) they are alike, equal, not differing, col.; with daṅ (dang), seldom with the termin., lha (lha) daṅ (dang) mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) yod (yod) they are like unto the gods Pth., Glr.; zlúm-por (zlum por) mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) roundish Sambh.; rigs (rigs) mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) of equal birth, rank Dzl.; dus (dus) mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) contemporary, simultaneous, frq. mnyám-par (mnyam par) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) to become equal, to be equal Dzl. — 2. even, level, flat, lag-mtíl (lag mthil) ltár (ltar) mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) flat like the palm of the hand Glr. and elsewh.; mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) (or -par (-par)) byéd-pa (byed pa) to make even or level, to even, to equalize Dzl.; to divide equally; sems (sems) mnyám-pa (mnyam pa) imperturbation, evenness of mind, not to be affected by kindness or the reverse; sems (sems) mnyám-par (mnyam par) jóg-pa ('jog pa) to compose the
